title: Binance signed-URL with HMAC signature now logged on every fapi network failure (regression vs. pre-sweep silent path)
summary: Pre-sweep, the bare `except: pass` around the Binance `fapiPrivate_get_income` block silently swallowed network-class ccxt failures (`RequestTimeout`, `ExchangeNotAvailable`, `ExchangeError` from `aiohttp.ClientError`). Post-sweep, the new `logger.warning('Binance futures-income failed (falling back to BTC spot trades): %s', exc, exc_info=True)` writes the exception's `str(exc)` into Railway stdout AND into Sentry breadcrumbs. For ccxt's network exception class, the exception message is constructed at `ccxt/async_support/base/exchange.py:271-285` as `details = ' '.join([self.id, method, url])` — and for Binance signed GETs (which fapiPrivate_get_income is), the URL is built at `ccxt/async_support/binance.py:11538-11543` as `url += '?' + query` where `query` ends with `&signature=<HEX-HMAC-SHA256>`. The new WARNING therefore emits the HMAC-SHA256 signature on every Binance fapi timeout/DNS/connection-reset. The pre-existing `validate_key_permissions` exception handlers log only ccxt.AuthenticationError-class exceptions (which carry the response body, NOT the URL) — this sweep is the FIRST site to expose network-class URLs on a recurring (cron-scheduled) sync path. Defense-in-depth violation: HMAC signature is not the secret itself, but a known-plaintext + MAC pair lands in logs.
evidence: ccxt source `analytics-service/.venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/ccxt/async_support/base/exchange.py` lines 271-285: `details = ' '.join([self.id, method, url])` for all 4 network-class exceptions. ccxt source `analytics-service/.venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/ccxt/async_support/binance.py` line 11538: `query += '&' + 'signature=' + signature`. Line 11543: `url += '?' + query`. Sentry breadcrumb format at `sentry-sdk/integrations/logging.py:369-377` stores `record.message` (the formatted log string with signature) into `breadcrumb['message']` field — and `analytics-service/sentry_init.py:_redact_before_send` only scrubs `breadcrumbs[*]['data']`, NOT `breadcrumbs[*]['message']`. Confirmed by reading sentry_init.py L224-L232.
fix: Two options, low-overhead: (1) redact `exc` via `services.redact.scrub_freeform_string(str(exc))` before logging, OR (2) log only `type(exc).__name__` plus a sanitized message. Recommended concrete change: replace `logger.warning('Binance futures-income failed (falling back to BTC spot trades): %s', exc, exc_info=True)` with `logger.warning('Binance futures-income failed (falling back to BTC spot trades), exc_class=%s', type(exc).__name__, exc_info=True)`. Python's `traceback.format_exception` (the implementation behind `exc_info=True`) DOES emit the exception's `__str__` as the first line — so even with the format-arg change, the signature would still appear in the traceback's first line. Stronger fix: catch network-class ccxt errors specifically (`ccxt.NetworkError`) and log only the exchange.id, suppressing the traceback's str(exc) line via a custom formatter. Minimal acceptable: at minimum apply `services.redact.scrub_freeform_string` to `str(exc)` before logging AND drop `exc_info=True` for the network-class branch.

title: Bybit closed_pnl WARNING also passes `exc_info=True` — but Bybit V5 puts signature in headers, not URL
summary: Same `logger.warning('Bybit closed_pnl fetch / ISO-conversion failed: %s', exc, exc_info=True)` pattern as the Binance site. For ccxt Bybit V5 (this branch calls `private_get_v5_position_closed_pnl`), the signing scheme places HMAC in the `X-BAPI-SIGN` HTTP HEADER, not in the URL — see `ccxt/async_support/bybit.py` sign() method's `isV5UnifiedAccount` branch. The URL `?category=linear&limit=200` contains no secrets. Therefore: NOT a credential leak for the V5 path. However, the WARNING DOES still expose: (a) the exception class and (b) any response body string that ccxt placed in the exception (Bybit error bodies sometimes include retCode/retMsg with no PII). Lower confidence because the V5 surface is benign as-coded; the finding is preventative for a future refactor that switches to a legacy Bybit path (which DOES embed api_key+timestamp+sign in URL — see `ccxt/async_support/bybit.py` else-branch of sign()).
evidence: `ccxt/async_support/bybit.py` sign() function: V5 isUnifiedAccount branch builds `url += '?' + queryEncoded` from `params` only; signature lives in `headers['X-BAPI-SIGN']`. The else-branch (non-V5/non-OpenAPI legacy) DOES embed `'api_key': self.apiKey, 'recv_window': ..., 'timestamp': ..., 'sign': signature` into the URL via `keysort(query)` + rawencode — that path is not used here today. The current `private_get_v5_position_closed_pnl` call is safe.
fix: Defensive: same redaction pattern as the Binance fix above. Not blocking — apply if/when applying the Binance fix as one PR. Optionally pin a test that asserts the call site is `private_get_v5_position_closed_pnl` (so a future migration to a legacy Bybit endpoint surfaces here).

title: Sentry `before_send` scrubber walks `breadcrumbs[*]['data']` only — `['message']` field is un-scrubbed (pre-existing gap, sweep's new WARNINGs land in it)
summary: Pre-existing defense-in-depth gap, not introduced by this sweep, but the sweep's new fail-loud WARNINGs now ride through it. The Sentry before_send hook scrubs `breadcrumbs[*]['data']` (line 232: `crumb['data'] = _scrub(crumb['data'])`) but does NOT touch `breadcrumbs[*]['message']` — which is where Sentry's LoggingIntegration puts `record.getMessage()` (the format-string-interpolated WARNING text). The Binance signed-URL finding above lands here: when a downstream ERROR-level event triggers a Sentry capture, the prior WARNING breadcrumb (with the signed URL in its `message`) attaches to the event and is sent to Sentry un-scrubbed. The sweep is the first reason to fix this gap, but the fix belongs in `sentry_init.py`, not in the sweep.
evidence: `analytics-service/sentry_init.py` lines 227-232: walks `event['breadcrumbs']['values'][*]['data']` only. `sentry-sdk/integrations/logging.py:370-376` shows the breadcrumb dict shape: `{'type': 'log', 'level': ..., 'category': ..., 'message': record.message, 'timestamp': ..., 'data': self._extra_from_record(record)}` — message is a top-level breadcrumb field, not under data.
fix: In `_redact_before_send`, extend the breadcrumbs walker to also scrub `crumb.get('message')` via `services.redact.scrub_freeform_string(crumb['message'])` (which applies the `key: value` substring redactor — line 80-90 of redact.py: matches `api[-_]?key`, `api[-_]?secret`, `signature`, `token`, etc.). This is a separate PR — sweep should not change Sentry scrubbing surface.

title: `logger.warning(..., exc_info=True)` traceback frame summaries do NOT include local variable values by default — partial mitigation
summary: Defense-in-depth note. Python's `logging.Formatter.formatException` → `traceback.format_exception` emits the exception chain + stack frame summaries (filename, lineno, code line) WITHOUT local variable values. So `exc_info=True` does not directly leak ccxt's `apiKey`/`secret` even though the `exchange` object lives in the failing frame's locals. The leak surface is purely `str(exc)` (the first line of the exc_info output). Sentry's `with_locals=True` (the SDK default; `send_default_pii=False` at `sentry_init.py:293` is orthogonal) would attach frame.vars on event-level captures — but WARNINGs are breadcrumbs only (LoggingIntegration default `event_level=ERROR`), so frame.vars are NOT serialized for the sweep's new WARNINGs. The outer fetch_daily_pnl wrapper's `logger.error('fetch_daily_pnl failed: %s', str(e))` at L620-L621 DOES use ERROR-level — but it logs `str(e)` only (no exc_info), so Sentry would capture an event with auto-collected frame.vars and the `_redact_before_send` PII scrubber at sentry_init.py:237-251 walks `exception[*].stacktrace.frames[*].vars` and `_scrub`s them — coverage is good for the `exchange` object (its `__repr__` returns just `ccxt.async_support.binance()`, no apiKey leak — see `ccxt/base/exchange.py:486-487`).
evidence: Python 3 stdlib `traceback.format_exception()`: does not emit locals by default. `sentry_init.py:237-251` walks frame.vars and applies `_scrub`. `ccxt/base/exchange.py:486-487` shows Exchange `__repr__` returns only `'ccxt.' + ('async_support.' if ...) + self.id + '()'`.
fix: None required for sweep — existing PII-scrub coverage for the outer ERROR is correct. Document only.

title: Cron / authenticated entry points throttle log volume — DoS-to-log-sink risk bounded
summary: Mandate question: a previously-best-effort path now logs — can an attacker trigger the WARNING repeatedly to overwhelm Railway/Sentry sinks? Reviewed entry points: `routers/exchange.py:131` (POST `/exchange/sync` requires JWT auth + strategy ownership check at L115-L116), `routers/cron.py:150` (cron-scheduled, INTERNAL_API_TOKEN gated at the cron handler), `routers/portfolio.py:992` (portfolio sync route, auth required), `services/job_worker.py:529` (background worker, no external trigger). Authenticated triggers + cron rate-limit the volume. Sentry has 0.1 traces_sample_rate at `sentry_init.py:292` which damps event volume; breadcrumb volume is unaffected by traces_sample_rate (breadcrumbs only attach when an event fires). Railway's stdout-log buffer is generous. No realistic DoS path to log sink from this sweep alone.
evidence: routers/exchange.py:115-116: `if api_key_row.data.get('user_id') != strategy_result.data.get('user_id'): raise HTTPException(status_code=403, ...)`. routers/cron.py:13 imports `validate_internal_token` (INTERNAL_API_TOKEN gate). sentry_init.py:292: traces_sample_rate=0.1.

title: `fetch_daily_pnl` outer ERROR cascade fires only when both primary and fallback paths fail — no auth-bypass info leak
summary: Mandate question: any of the new raises happen BEFORE an auth check, allowing an unauthenticated client to glean information from the error response? Reviewed: `fetch_daily_pnl` is called only AFTER auth in `routers/exchange.py` (JWT + strategy ownership at L115-L116), `routers/cron.py` (INTERNAL_API_TOKEN), `routers/portfolio.py` (auth), and `services/job_worker.py` (no external trigger). The outer wrapper at L620 returns `daily_pnl = []` on cascade — never propagates `str(e)` to an HTTP response. Routes that call `fetch_all_trades` (which calls fetch_daily_pnl) wrap with their own `try/except` → `raise HTTPException(500, detail='Failed to fetch trades from exchange')` (routers/exchange.py:133-134). The detail is a generic English string, not the underlying exc message. No info leak to client.
evidence: routers/exchange.py:130-134: `try: trades = await fetch_all_trades(...) ... except Exception: raise HTTPException(500, detail='Failed to fetch trades from exchange')`. exchange.py:620-621: `except Exception as e: logger.error('fetch_daily_pnl failed: %s', str(e))` — log only, no return propagation.

title: metrics.py WARNINGs interpolate `exc` + `returns_len` only — no user-controlled strings, no PII surface
summary: Reviewed all 11 new `logger.warning(...)` calls in compute_all_metrics. Format args are: scalar name (literal string), `returns_len_for_log` (int), `len(monthly_rets)` (int), and `exc` (a qstats/pandas/numpy exception). The `returns` Series carries float values from analytics_runner (post-decryption, post-position-reconstruction — no API keys / user emails / encryption_key / KEK). qstats/pandas/numpy exception messages are operationally bounded (e.g. 'cannot convert NaN to integer', 'array empty', 'operands could not be broadcast'). No user-controlled string interpolation, no f-string injection, no PII surface. Confirmed by reading metrics.py:225-572 and analytics_runner.py:1142-1143.
evidence: All 11 sites at metrics.py:350-353, 358-361, 384-388, 392-395, 399-403, 407-411, 415-419, 423-427, 431-434, 439-442, 470-472, 564-568 use `returns_len_for_log, exc, exc_info=True` (or +`len(monthly_rets)` for var_1m_99 at L387). `compute_all_metrics(returns, benchmark_returns)` signature at L225 — both args are pandas Series of floats.

title: `logger.exception` pattern in `validate_key_permissions` already established — sweep extends but does not regress the surface
summary: Mandate question: does any new fail-loud site log data that previously didn't appear in logs? Reviewed: the existing `validate_key_permissions` handlers at L274-L342 use `logger.exception('... on %s — %s', exchange.id, exc)` for AuthenticationError, DDoSProtection, RateLimitExceeded, ExchangeNotAvailable, NetworkError. These ccxt classes' messages are constructed from response BODY (`feedback = self.id + ' ' + body` in binance.py:11616/11626/11628) — NOT the signed URL. So the pre-existing `logger.exception` calls don't expose the URL. The NEW sweep's `except Exception` (catch-all) at exchange.py:552 (Binance) catches BOTH response-body-class errors AND network-class errors (`RequestTimeout` etc), the latter of which DO embed the URL. This is the regression captured by Finding #1. Pre-existing risk: bounded to response-body content (operationally benign). New risk: extends to URL+signature.
evidence: exchange.py:274-342 shows existing pattern. ccxt/async_support/binance.py:11616-11628 shows feedback construction without URL. ccxt/async_support/base/exchange.py:271-285 shows network-class exception construction WITH URL.
fix: See Finding #1 (Binance) for the remediation. Bybit V5 (Finding #2) is benign as-coded.
